decks.notes: The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a combination of fast mana acceleration, disruptive artifacts, and efficient threats. Mishra's Workshop allows for explosive starts, while cards like Sphere of Resistance and Trinisphere slow down the opponent's game plan. Golos, Tireless Pilgrim provides card advantage and ramp, while Karn, the Great Creator can shut down opposing strategies. The deck also includes win conditions like Stonecoil Serpent and Patchwork Automaton to close out the game quickly. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying powerful threats to secure victory.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent with counterspells and removal while applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Deathrite Shaman and Tarmogoyf. It also utilizes cards like Leovold, Emissary of Trest and Collector Ouphe to control the game and slow down the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to maintain board presence and card advantage through efficient card draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. It also has access to powerful mana acceleration with cards like Black Lotus and the Moxen, allowing for explosive plays and a strong midrange game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Oath of Druids card to bring out powerful creatures quickly, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier. It also utilizes control elements like Counterspells, Artifact Hate (Null Rod), and disruptive cards like Oko, Thief of Crowns to slow down the opponent. Additionally, the deck includes card draw (Ancestral Recall, Ponder, Brainstorm) and tutor effects (Vampiric Tutor, Demonic Tutor) to find key cards efficiently. The deck aims to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition with powerful creatures.

This deck focuses on utilizing Paradoxical Outcome to generate card advantage and mana acceleration, allowing for explosive plays and combos. The deck includes powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Moxen, and Tolarian Academy to generate mana quickly. It also includes countermagic like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its combo pieces. The addition of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional recursion and flexibility in gameplay. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and abilities.

The deck focuses on aggressive early plays to gain tempo advantage, utilizing a combination of efficient threats and disruptive spells to control the board and pressure the opponent. It aims to quickly establish board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan, utilizing powerful spells like Force of Will and disruptive creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to maintain control. By efficiently managing resources and manipulating the game state, the deck seeks to close out the game quickly while preventing the opponent from stabilizing.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent with counter spells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm, while also utilizing disruptive creatures like Collector Ouphe and Leovold, Emissary of Trest. It aims to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, while also digging for key cards with cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm. The deck also utilizes powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time to generate card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure while disrupting the opponent's game plan in a midrange aggressive strategy.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts, while also disrupting their strategy with cards like Wasteland and Trinisphere. The deck focuses on mana acceleration and resource denial, utilizing cards like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith to cast big threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The deck also has ways to untap artifacts like Time Vault and Mana Crypt with cards like Manifold Key, enabling powerful combos.Overall, the deck aims to quickly build a threatening board presence and control the game through disruptive artifacts and efficient creatures.

This deck focuses on utilizing hatebear creatures to disrupt the opponent's plays while applying aggressive pressure. Cards like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex slow down the opponent's spells, giving you an advantage. Anointed Peacekeeper and Clarion Conqueror help control the board while Seasoned Dungeoneer and White Plume Adventurer provide card advantage. The inclusion of hate cards like Void Mirror and Karn, the Great Creator further hinder the opponent's game plan. With efficient mana acceleration and disruptive creatures, this deck aims to outvalue and outpace the opponent in a Vintage 1 vs 1 match.

The deck focuses on using powerful control cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Counterspells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage with cards like Dig Through Time and Ancestral Recall. It also utilizes efficient creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Orcish Bowmasters to pressure the opponent and close out the game. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Moxen, and Urza's Saga allows for explosive starts and additional resources. The deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent to ensure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with dredge creatures and triggers to bring back powerful creatures like Prized Amalgam and Ichorid. The deck aims to mill cards from its own library using cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Creeping Chill, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Grief and Force of Will. By combining graveyard synergy and control elements, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful dredge combo.

The key concept of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with aggressive artifacts and creatures, while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void. The deck utilizes powerful mana acceleration cards like Mishra's Workshop and Mana Crypt to quickly deploy threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The inclusion of cards like Manifold Key and Time Vault allows for additional combo potential, while cards like Soul-Guide Lantern and Pithing Needle provide disruption and control options. Overall, the deck aims to be fast, disruptive, and versatile in its strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements such as counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage through powerful draw spells. It relies on a mix of efficient interaction and tempo plays to control the board and outvalue the opponent, ultimately leading to a victory through attrition. Additionally, the inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional resilience and grindiness in longer games, further emphasizing the deck's control-oriented game plan.

This combo deck aims to quickly and efficiently fill up the graveyard with cards like Ichorid, Stinkweed Imp, and Golgari Thug, while utilizing cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder to dig for combo pieces. Once the graveyard is filled, the deck can trigger abilities like Creeping Chill and Prized Amalgam to deal damage and bring back creatures from the graveyard. The deck also includes disruption spells like Force of Will and Mindbreak Trap to protect the combo and control the game.

The deck aims to generate a large amount of mana quickly using fast mana artifacts like Moxen and Ancient Tomb, along with cards like Grim Monolith and Sol Ring. Paradoxical Outcome is used to draw a large number of cards while also returning those mana artifacts to your hand to generate even more mana. The deck utilizes Time Vault and Voltaic Key or Manifold Key to take extra turns, while also using Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator to tutor for key combo pieces. Force of Will and other counterspells are included to protect the combo.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble powerful artifacts, such as Coveted Jewel, Mana Vault, and Urza's Saga, in order to generate massive amounts of mana and draw cards. By utilizing cards like Tinker and Paradoxical Outcome, the deck aims to set up infinite loops and lock out the opponent with cards like Trinisphere and Force of Will. With access to powerful cards like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk, the deck can quickly gain an advantage and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive plays and disruptive spells.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of casting Doomsday to set up a winning sequence using Thassa's Oracle to win the game. The deck includes various cantrips and card selection to help find the combo pieces efficiently, as well as a mix of counterspells to protect the combo from disruption. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds additional value and flexibility to the deck's strategy.
